description: Apply a boundary condition to block gridpoints.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: Set a load at location v (for deformable blocks only).

keywords.name: force-x
keywords.description: Set a load in the \(x\) -direction.

keywords.name: force-y
keywords.description: Set a load in the \(y\) -direction.

keywords.name: force-z
keywords.description: Set a load in the \(z\) -direction.

keywords.name: quiet
keywords.description: Synonym for Viscous

keywords.name: quiet-x
keywords.description: Synonym for Viscous-X.

keywords.name: quiet-y
keywords.description: Synonym for Viscous-Y.

keywords.name: quiet-z
keywords.description: Synonym for Viscous-Z

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Set the velocity (for deformable blocks only).

keywords.name: velocity-normal
keywords.description: WARNING: This command requires that a whole face containing the vertex be within range. Unexpected results may occur depending on how the range is defined. Consider using block face apply instead.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: Set the \(x\) -direction velocity (for deformable blocks only).

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: Set the \(y\) -direction velocity (for deformable blocks only).

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: Set the \(z\) -direction velocity (for deformable blocks only).

keywords.name: viscous
keywords.description: Set a viscous boundary in all direction on the specified range. Note Viscous boundaries cannot be used together with a velocity boundary. Viscous boundaries may be combined with stress boundaries. Nonreflecting boundaries are available for dynamic analyses.

keywords.name: viscous-x
keywords.description: Set a nonreflecting boundary in the \(x\) -direction.

keywords.name: viscous-y
keywords.description: Set a nonreflecting boundary in the \(y\) -direction.

keywords.name: viscous-z
keywords.description: Set a nonreflecting boundary in the \(z\) -direction.

keywords.name: reaction
keywords.description: Set point loads to be applied in the opposite direction to unbalanced forces at all vertices within the range. The command model cycle 1 should be given before this command reaction so that unbalanced forces are calculated. Any velocity boundary conditions will be removed when this command is applied.

keywords.name: reaction-x
keywords.description: Set point loads to be applied in the opposite \(x\) -direction.

keywords.name: reaction-y
keywords.description: Set point loads to be applied in the opposite \(y\) -direction.

keywords.name: reaction-z
keywords.description: Set point loads to be applied in the opposite \(z\) -direction.

keywords.name: source
keywords.description: A heat-generating source, f , is applied as a point source of the specified strength (e.g., in \(W\) ) at each gridpoint in the specified range. When a new source is applied to a gridpoint with an existing source, the new source strength is added to the existing source strength.

keywords.name: temperature
keywords.description: Temperature is fixed to f at all gridpoints in the specified range. The fish or table keyword can be used to prescribe a temperature history. (See below.)

keywords.name: kwd:fish
keywords.description: The specified FISH symbol is used to provide a multiplier.

keywords.name: kwd:table
keywords.description: is the multiplier given by the TABLE command. TABLE n consists of a list of pairs: time, f(time). Linear interpolation is performed between the given discrete points.

keywords.name: kwd:verbose
keywords.description: Increase messages to screen. The default setting is false .
